title: Natural resource management directory entry
description: One organization per object. Categorized for map UI (NOAA-style US regions, state) and display filters (Fisheries & aquatic science, Wildlife & land, Conservation NGO, State agency).

id.description: Stable, unique key for routing, diffs, and deduping (kebab-case, no spaces). Never change once published.

name.description: Official organization name as shown on the site or charter.

category.description: Primary pillar for the directory. Pick the one that best matches the org's core statutory or mission focus.

summary.description: 1–3 sentences: why it belongs here and how it advances natural resource management (NRM). Used in cards; write for a general audience.

careers_url.description: Direct employment / careers / jobs page. If omitted, the UI may use `url` for the link button (document that behavior in the app).

location.description: Geography for the map. US entries must be placeable in NOAA-style regions and/or states.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (e.g. US, CA, GB). Use US for U.S. organizations.

region.description: NOAA Fisheries–style key for the existing US map. Required when country is US. Use `international` only when the listing is not tied to a specific US on-map placement (e.g. truly global HQs) — prefer state/region for US offices when known.

state.description: USPS 2-letter state/territory code (e.g. WA, DC, PR) when a single home base applies.

states.description: Use when the org operates across multiple US jurisdictions (e.g. regional project space). Map filtering matches if the selected state is in this list.

us_national.description: If true, the listing is treated as US-wide (e.g. national NGO); show when any US state in the org's NOAA region is selected, or as defined in your UI.

tags.description: Optional facet tags for future filters (e.g. salmon, easements, water_quality).

organization.description: Optional duplicate of `name` for legacy UIs; prefer `name`.

provenance.description: How and when the row was vetted. Recommended for 'real data' curation.
